Video.summary: Vicki Lawrence guests with an all-singing, all-dancing number. Then, a spoof of the film "Love Story."
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guest star Lucille Ball joins Carol for a glittering showbiz sketch, "The Rock Sisters!"
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Lucille Ball, George Carlin

Video.summary: Guest Neil Sedaka joins the gang for a set of commercial parodies.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Neil Sedaka

Video.summary: The season-ending "Family Show" features the best variety show ensemble on television.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Joel Grey & Cass Elliot guests! Features a salute to Foreign Language films, and a sketch with puppies.
Video.title: Joel Grey, Cass Elliot

Video.summary: Cass Elliot and Ricardo Montalban guest. Cass joins a sketch about wallflowers, Ricardo takes a playboy role.
Video.title: Ricardo Montalban, Cass Elliot

Video.summary: Sketches include an escaped convict seeking refuge in a diner, plus Carol and Vicki as robots.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: John Byner, Francine Beers

Video.summary: It's time to say "so long" as Carol closes the book on one of the greatest shows in television history.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Carol plays a housewife obsessed with celebrity gossip. Then, a take-off on a classic with "Rebecky."
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence and Carol Channing join Carol, Harvey, Vicki, and Lyle for a tribute to the Academy Awards.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Carol Channing, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Nanette Fabray and Steve Lawrence guest star in an episode saluting Columbia Pictures classic movies.
Video.title: Nanette Fabray, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence guests! Includes Mr. Tudball attempting to explain the new intercom to secretary Mrs. Wiggins.
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence & Tim Conway star! Steve plays a talk show host, then solves a mystery in a spoof with Tim.
Video.title: Tim Conway, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Tim Conway steals Carol's heart in a sketch about two shoppers browsing waterbeds.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Tim Conway, Diahann Carroll

Video.summary: Ken Berry joins a salute to Warner Brothers, with spoofs of "Mrs. Skeffington" and "Yankee Doodle Dandy."
Video.title: Ken Berry

Video.summary: Jonathan Winters guests and is interviewed by Harvey. In "Carol & Sis", Carol and Harvey struggle with names.
Video.title: Jonathan Winters

Video.summary: Burt Reynolds and Nanette Fabray join the gang for a series of Carol's patented commercial parodies.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Nanette Fabray, Burt Reynolds

Video.summary: Guest Tim Conway is featured in two sketches, pairing him with Harvey and Carol, respectively.
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Guests Ronnie Schell and Vicki Carr take to the stage with stand-up and song (respectively)!
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Ronnie Schell, Vikki Carr

Video.summary: Carol is a dowdy secretary discouraging applicants, and the movie spoof "Flight 13 To Nowhere."
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Nanette Fabray, Ken Berry

Video.summary: David Hartman & Paula Kelly star! Features Nora Desmond and George and Zelda. Also: "This Is Your Lifetime."
Video.title: David Hartman, Paula Kelly

Video.summary: Rock Hudson and Nancy Walker join Carol and company for sketch entitled: "When My Baby Laughs At Me."
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Rock Hudson, Nancy Walker

Video.summary: Jim Nabors returns for his final season premiere! Also, new member Dick Van Dyke joins Tim for a pantomime.
Video.title: Jim Nabors

Video.summary: Guest Gloria Swanson joins the Q&A. Sketches include "Carol & Sis" where the Bradfords discourage an operator.
Video.title: Gloria Swanson

Video.summary: Bernadette Peters and Cass Elliot guest star in "As The Stomach Turns", and Lyle causes havoc and absurdity.
Video.title: Bernadette Peters, Cass Elliot

Video.summary: Tim and Carol spar over a call, Tim tries to sell Vicki a vacuum, and the gang performs a musical recital.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guest Tim Conway appears in a sketch where two policemen go undercover. Also: a poker game on "Carol & Sis."
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Guest star Tim Conway steals the show as a bank robber, and as a conventioneer double booked in a hotel room.
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Alan King stars as a psychiatrist. Also: Carol tries making breakfast; a nurse & patient recognize each other.
Video.title: Alan King

Video.summary: Scoey Mitchell is Carol's guest. Featuring "The Queen Elizabeth Show", a Q&A and a "Queen & Sis" sketch.
Video.title: Scoey Mitchell

Video.summary: Ken Berry and Shirley Jones star. Carol is under a spell and Shirley joins "As The Stomach Turns."
Video.title: Shirley Jones, Ken Berry

Video.summary: Carl Reiner guests. Featuring WWII film "The Plot To Hurt Hitler." Also: "The Writer" & "Terminal Hospital."
Video.title: Carl Reiner

Video.summary: Mr. Steve Lawrence joins Carol in a sketch about a traveling couple sharing some honesty with each other.
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Features a take-off of the classic film "National Velvet", and a sketch with Carol & Harvey as a couple.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Features "Carol & Sis", where Harvey avoids Carol, and a take-off on "Back Street"..here called "Back Alley."
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guests Robert Goulet and Imogene Coca appear in a fairy tale musical--the story of Cinderrumplewhite.
Video.title: Imogene Coca, Robert Goulet

Video.summary: David Frost and Marilyn Horne star. Frost plays a Rolls Royce dealer, while Horne takes the musical spotlight.
Video.title: Marilyn Horne, David Frost
